mnemonics: B.I.N. Add-Sub: Borrow? Invert, Negate. (For subtraction, you Borrow the carry-in, Invert the second number, and set the carry-in to 1 to effectively Negate it.)
keyPoints: A binary adder-subtractor circuit uses a single adder module to perform both addition and subtraction.
The mode input (M) controls the operation: M=0 for addition (B passes through), M=1 for subtraction (B is inverted using XOR gates).
For subtraction (M=1), the initial carry-in (C0) is set to 1 to complete the two's complement conversion (invert + 1).
commonMistakes: Forgetting to set the initial carry-in (C0) to 1 when performing subtraction (M=1), which results in a one's complement operation instead of two's complement.
Misinterpreting the overflow flag; it indicates that the result exceeds the representable range for the given number of bits, not simply that a carry-out occurred.
